project.name: Legacy IT Student Portal (LCC IT Institutional System)
project.description: A web-based institutional portal for the LCC Information Technology Department. The primary module is QR-code-based class attendance, built on an expandable modular architecture that will grow into a full student portal (grades, clearance, announcements, laboratory borrowing, events, and more).
project.vision: One unified, modern, and lightweight portal where IT students, faculty, and administrators manage all academic day-to-day interactions, starting with fast and fraud-resistant attendance.
goals: Replace manual/paper attendance with secure QR-code scanning
Give faculty real-time visibility of who is present, late, or absent
Give students self-service access to their own attendance records
Provide admins with accurate, exportable reports and analytics
Establish a modular codebase where future modules plug in without rewrites
Keep the system light enough to run on modest hosting while remaining secure
scope: Authentication and role-based access control (5 roles)
Student, faculty, subject, and section management (CRUD)
QR code generation (per class session) and scanning (student device or kiosk)
Attendance recording, monitoring, correction, and reporting

Super Admin: Manage system settings, academic years, and semesters
Department Admin: Manage students, faculty, subjects, sections, and schedules within the IT department
Faculty: Start/end class sessions and generate session QR codes
View live attendance for own classes
Manually override attendance status (with reason, logged)
View and export reports for own subjects/sections
Student: Scan QR codes to log attendance
View own attendance history and summaries
Guest: View public landing page and general information only

authentication.method: Students: single sign-on with university credentials via the OSAS API (no separate registration — accounts already exist in OSAS); Faculty/Admin: local email + password accounts provisioned by admins

osas_integration.account_source: Student accounts are provisioned by connecting to the OSAS API where students already have accounts
osas_integration.verification: Credentials and student status are verified against the university database through the OSAS API on every login
osas_integration.eligibility: Only current, active students may log in — alumni and inactive students are rejected based on OSAS status

authentication.tokens: JWT access token (15 min) + rotating refresh token (7 days) in httpOnly secure cookies, issued after OSAS/local verification
authentication.account_lockout: 5 failed attempts locks the account for 15 minutes
authentication.password_reset: Local accounts: email-based reset link with single-use token (30 min expiry); student credentials are managed by OSAS
authentication.two_factor: Optional email OTP for admin roles

authorization.model: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) enforced server-side via middleware
authorization.route_guards: Frontend route guards mirror backend RBAC for UX only; backend is the source of truth
authorization.resource_ownership: Faculty limited to own classes; students limited to own records

student.student_id_format: Follows the university OSAS format (e.g. YYYY-NNNNN)
sections: Defined per year level and course, e.g. BSIT-3A, BSIT-3B

attendance.attendance_method: Student scans a rotating session QR code displayed by the faculty (projector/screen); fallback kiosk mode where faculty scans student digital IDs

absence_policy.auto_mark_absent: Students not scanned by session end are marked Absent automatically
absence_policy.excused_requires: Faculty or admin approval with attached reason/document
absence_policy.warning_threshold: 3 consecutive or 20% total absences triggers notification to student and adviser

attendance.multiple_scan_policy: Students may retry scanning within a short time frame (failed camera reads, technical issues); only the first VALID scan is recorded — duplicates are ignored and logged, so each student is marked present at most once per session

location_verification.note: Attendance relies on the dynamic QR code alone; no GPS or room verification

attendance.qr_expiration: QR payload rotates every 60 seconds (1 minute); session QR valid only while the class/event session is open
scan_validation: Session must be open (between start and cutoff time)
Rotating token must be current (60s window, 1 window tolerance)
Student must be enrolled in the class (or registered for the event)
Student has not already been marked for this session (duplicate prevention)
Server timestamp is authoritative (client time ignored)

attendance_window.closes: At session end or when faculty closes the session manually

qr.generation: Server-generated, one QR per class or event session; rendered client-side from an encrypted payload
qr.rotation: Dynamic QR that regenerates every 60 seconds (1 minute); each new code is unique and valid only for its window
qr.static_option: Static QR available for specific events/classes where dynamic codes are impractical — admin-enabled per session, flagged as reduced-security

security.payload_encryption: AES-256-GCM encrypted payload with server-held key (data cannot be read or tampered with by clients)
security.payload_integrity: GCM auth tag + server-side token validation (rotating HMAC-derived token)
security.replay_protection: Single-use per student per session; timestamp + token window validated server-side
security.screenshot_mitigation: 1-minute rotation plus server timestamp/validity checks mean shared screenshots expire almost immediately

qr.encoding: Encrypted compact payload: { sessionId (class/event ID), rotatingToken, issuedAt } encoded as QR (error correction level M)
validation: Duplicate check (one record per student per session)

subjects.subject_code: e.g. IT301, CC104 — unique per curriculum
subjects.subject_name: Full descriptive title, e.g. Web Systems and Technologies
schedule: One or more meeting blocks: day + start time + end time + room + type (Lecture/Lab)
subjects.assigned_faculty: One primary faculty per class offering (subject + section + term)

meeting_times: 07:00-21:00 range, scheduled in blocks (e.g. 1.5h lecture, 3h laboratory)
classes.schedule_source: Weekly schedules can be pulled from the OSAS API; calendar exceptions (holidays, class suspensions, makeup classes, special events) are synced or managed locally and automatically suppress/add sessions

permissions: Manage sessions for assigned classes only
assigned_subjects: Assigned per term by Department Admin via class offerings

student_dashboard: Today's schedule with next class highlight
faculty_dashboard: Today's classes with quick 'Start Session' action
Live attendance count for the active session
admin_dashboard: Top absent students and low-attendance classes

attendance_reports: Daily attendance sheet per class session
SF-style term attendance summary for records

session_management.concurrent_sessions: Allowed; each device tracked and revocable

encryption.at_rest: Database-level encryption (provider) + argon2id password hashes + HMAC-signed QR payloads
encryption.secrets: Environment variables via .env (never committed); rotated per environment

backup.database: Automated daily backup, 30-day retention, weekly offsite copy

relationships: users 1:1 students | users 1:1 faculty (role profile tables)
subjects 1:N class_offerings (per term); class_offerings N:1 faculty
constraints: One attendance record per student per session
Enrollment required before attendance insert (FK + service check)

api.authentication: JWT Bearer (access) + httpOnly refresh cookie; /auth/refresh rotation endpoint
routes: /api/v1/integrations/osas (student sync, schedule sync, calendar exceptions, connection health)

frontend.routing: React Router v6 (lazy-loaded route modules per feature)
frontend.ui_library: Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui (Radix primitives), lucide-react icons, Recharts for charts, html5-qrcode for scanning
frontend.theme: Light/dark mode with institutional color palette; responsive mobile-first layout

backend.architecture: Layered modular monolith: routes → controllers → services → repositories (Prisma), organized per feature module
backend.storage: Local disk in dev; S3-compatible object storage (e.g. Cloudflare R2) for photos/exports in production

deployment.hosting: Single VPS (Docker Compose: nginx + api + postgres) or PaaS (Railway/Render) — both supported; frontend served as static build via nginx or Vercel

environment.development: Local, Docker Compose, seeded data, hot reload
environment.production: HTTPS, backups, monitoring, PM2/Docker restart policies

integrations: OSAS API (core, Phase 1): student accounts/SSO, profiles, schedules, calendar exceptions
